LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS FIRST, LAST & ALWAYS, INC. AND DEVEN GADULA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. A R G U E D - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATIVE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. MS. KNIGHT'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IS NOT INADMISSIBLE PER THE D'AMICO RULE. NOR IS MS. KNIGHT'S TESTIMONY BEFORE THE CUIAB INADMISSIBLE. ALL OBJECTIONS TO MS. KNIGHT'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND HER TESTIMONY BEFORE THE CUIAB ARE THEREFORE OVERRULED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES ON EACH OF MS. KNIGHT'S CLAIMS. SPECIFICALLY, THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER MS. KNIGHT'S WORK ENVIRONMENT WAS SO INTOLERABLE THAT SHE WAS CONSTRUCTIVELY DISCHARGED AND WHETHER MR. GADULA HAD KNOWLEDGE OF THE ASSERTEDLY INTOLERABLE WORK ENVIRONMENT. AMONG THE EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT CREATES THESE TRIABLE ISSUES ARE THE INCIDENTS OF LEERING, INAPPROPRIATE PHYSICAL CONTACT, CATCALLS, REPEATED UNWELCOME REQUESTS TO GO OUT ON DATES, REFERENCES TO MR. KNIGHT AS A HIGH SCHOOL GIRL AND TO MALE EMPLOYEES AS MS. KNIGHT'S BOYFRIENDS, PORNOGRAPHY AT WORK, AND INSINUATIONS THAT MR. KNIGHT BROUGHT A VIBRATOR TO WORK AND THAT SHE CLOGGED A BATHROOM WITH FEMININE HYGIENE PRODUCTS. BECAUSE TRUJILLO REMAINS GOOD LAW, THERE IS NO LEGAL IMPEDIMENT TO MS. KNIGHT PURSING RELIEF PER 12940(K). PLAINTIFF SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH BOTH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-28-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-12-2015 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-05-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. JORDAN A. SUSSAN, ESQ. and AMANDA HAMILTON, ESQ. of Zief, Diment & Glickman, Tel No. 415-986-3644 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Attys. JOHN HONG, ESQ. and ALEKSANDR KATSNELSON, ESQ. og Littler Mendelson, P.C., Tel No. 415-399-8446 appearing for the Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS FIRST, LAST & ALWAYS, INC. AND DEVEN GADULA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication, Defendants' motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Ms. Knight's deposition testimony is not inadmissible per the D'Amico rule. Nor is Ms. Knight's testimony before the CUIAB inadmissible. All objections to Ms. Knight's deposition testimony and her testimony before the CUIAB are therefore overruled. There are triable issues on each of Ms. Knight's claims. Specifically, there are triable issues whether Ms. Knight's work environment was so intolerable that she was constructively discharged and whether Mr. Gadula had knowledge of the assertedly intolerable work environment. Among the evidence presented that creates these triable issues are the incidents of leering, inappropriate physical contact, catcalls, repeated unwelcome requests to go out on dates, references to Mr. Knight as a high school girl and to male employees as Ms. Knight's boyfriends, pornography at work, and insinuations that Mr. Knight brought a vibrator to work and that she clogged a bathroom with feminine hygiene products. Because Trujillo remains good law, there is no legal impediment to Ms. Knight pursing relief per 12940(k). Plaintiff shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with both CCP 437C(G) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)
